name: TARGET_COMBINATIONS_FOR_TOP_ASSIGNEE_TARGETPAIRS
description: Target combinations for top assignee target pairs. Auto-generated from the core-four benchmark and aligned to the validated query-frame Cypher semantics.
cypher:
MATCH (ymax:Year)
WITH max(ymax.year) AS max_year
MATCH (tp:TargetPair)<-[:HAS_TARGET_PAIR]-(p0:Patent)-[:PUBLISHED_IN]->(y0:Year)
WITH tp, min(y0.year) AS first_year, max_year
WHERE first_year >= max_year - $years + 1
MATCH (tp)<-[:HAS_TARGET_PAIR]-(p:Patent)-[:HAS_ASSIGNEE]->(a:Assignee)
WITH a, tp, count(DISTINCT p) AS patent_count
ORDER BY patent_count DESC
WITH a, collect(DISTINCT tp.name)[0..10] AS target_pairs, count(DISTINCT tp) AS emerging_targetpair_count
RETURN a.name AS assignee, emerging_targetpair_count, target_pairs
ORDER BY emerging_targetpair_count DESC, assignee
LIMIT 10

examples: For the novel combinations associated with those highly active companies, what exact target pairs do they include?
Which exact target-pair combinations are present among the novel combinations linked to those highly active companies?
What are the concrete target-pair identities behind the novel combinations of those highly active companies?
